title: The One With The Secret Closet

lines.text: The Hallway, Phoebe walks up to and knocks on Chandler and Monica's door.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (opens the door wearing a robe, but leaves the chain on) Hey, what's up?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I left my guitar here again.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, okay. No problem.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. (Monica closes the door, gets the guitar, and then hands it out.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Great! Umm...(Monica closes the door again and Phoebe knocks again.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So do you want to hang out or something?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe! You kinda caught me at a bad time.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh are you and Chandler...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes! Exactly.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. Do you guys want me to play for you?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (laughs) No. (Closes the door as Chandler walks up.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey Pheebs, what's up?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You ask an intriguing question Chandler Bing.
Chandler lets her into the apartment and reveals that Monica is getting a massage from another woman.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God! You're getting a massage! You never let me massage you!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, I can explain!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You told me you hate massages!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Buy stamps, pick up dry cleaning...Don't let Phoebe in.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can't believe this! How long as this been going on?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well umm, Alexandra has been...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, it has a name?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, don't get upset!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay-Oop! Too late! I'm leaving! Come on Chandler let's go! (She storms out.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, Phoebe I thought I'd-(Off Phoebe's look)-Yeah, what the hell. (Exits with Phoebe.)
Opening Credits
Joey's Bedroom, Joey is having a dream about Rachel giving birth with him as the father.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh okay, okay! One more push! One more push! Come on honey, we're almost there! We're almost there!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh Joey, I'm so happy things worked out for us that we're having this baby together. I love you so much.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, I love you too.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And I hope it's not an inappropriate time to say this but, you're the best sex I ever had.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's always appropriate! (Back to the matter at hand) Oh, okay. One more push! One more push!
Finally, the baby is delivered and starts crying.

lines.speaker: Doctor
lines.text: Oh, here is your beautiful baby. Congratulations!
She hands the baby to Joey who pulls down the blanket to reveal Ross's face where the baby's should be.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (crying) I hope you're a better father than you're a friend!! (Cries again and Joey wakes up in horror.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (bursts into the room) Joey! Joey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What? What's going on?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Come feel this! Come feel my belly!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Aaaah...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey! The baby is kicking for the first time! Will you please come feel this?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes!
Joey starts to get up but stops.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, y'know what? Maybe uh, you...you should come to me. I'm a not, I'm not wearing any bottoms.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, okay! (She goes over to him and he feels her belly.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my, oh my God!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Aw, it's unbelievable! Wow! She is kicking so much! Oh, she's like umm...oh...who's that kind of annoying girl soccer player?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Mia Hamm?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Mia Hamm!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh that's amazing. (Drops the sheet.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (looking down) Oh-oh!! One hand on the sheet Joe!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa-hey-oh! Sorry!
Monica and Chandler's, Monica has opened another wedding present as Chandler enters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey babe.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi honey. We just got a wedding gift from Bob and Faye Bing; they don't like us do they? (They gave them a pok-a-dotted punch bowl.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Who says you can't get a nice punch bowl for under six bucks? Maybe we can take it back?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, it doesn't say where it came from. Where would we return it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How about to the street say from the balcony?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why don't we just find a place for it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. How about in that cabinet?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! That's where we keep the canned goods! Have you completely forgotten everything you learned at orientation?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How about the closet by the bathroom? (The secret green door by the bathroom.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, okay. Here, why don't you let me do it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no-no-no, I will do it. Honey, you have to learn to sit down and relax and let your husband take care of things once and awhile. (Tries the door.) It's locked, you have to help me. Why is it locked?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No reason. I-I keep private things in there.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh y'know, I've been living here a while and I've never seen what's inside that closet. What is, what is in there?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Feminine stuff.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Don't try to make me uncomfortable with feminine stuff!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler, there is nothing in there that concerns you! If you love me you-you'll let it go.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Fine.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thank you! (He tries the door again and Monica glares at him.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (walking casually away) Love you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering) Hello Chandler, lovely day huh? (To Monica) You!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Phoebe if it helps Alexandra has only been massaging Monica for like three years. (Phoebe rolls her eyes and walks away and Monica glares at him.) If! I said, "If it helps!" (Goes to the bedroom.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why won't you let me massage you?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well it's...I mean I'd just-I'd be self-conscious. You're my friend; I'd be naked.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Monica! We lived together for years! I've seen you naked!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's different, we were roommates! And when?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm curious about the human body.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, come on Phoebe, you can understand why this would be weird for me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But I'm a professional! And I'm really good! Look, if you're uncomfortable we can stop. Just give me a chance, okay. Please?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, if it means that much to you...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It does! How would you feel if you couldn't share your cooking? Or-or imagine how Ross would feel if he couldn't teach us about dragons.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Dinosaurs.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Potato, potaato.
Central Perk, Joey and Rachel are there as Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Ross! The baby's kicking!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?! It is?! (He tries to quickly remove his gloves, but runs into trouble and finally throws them off of his hands like a hockey player in a fight and grabs Rachel's belly.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's not kicking right now. Although we would love to see you do (Mimics him) that again.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, when-when was it kicking? What happened?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Last night! I just felt it and I went into Joey's room and he was sleeping...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: A dreamless sleep.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: My God, the baby's kicking. That's great. Although I...kinda wish I-I would've been there to feel the kicking for the first time y'know.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well I got stuff going on in here (Rubbing his belly) if you wanna feel.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, I-I don't want to miss anymore baby stuff. So...Here. Here's my new pager number, okay? Anytime anything pregnancy related happens use it! I'll be there! Okay? I don't care if it's three in the morning and all you want is ice cream.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow! Can I get a copy of that?
Phoebe's Apartment, Monica is lying on the massage table waiting for Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (calling from her room) Are you under the sheet?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes.
Phoebe turns on some music and grabs some oil.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (in a soothing voice) Great, let's begin. (She starts the massage.) How's the pressure?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Nice! Wow Phoebe you are good!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Stating the obvious, but thank you. And it's not weird is it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No. Ooohh...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's right, you just enjoy.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (in a sexy voice) Oh. Oh yeah! Ohhhhh! Ohh! Oh yeah!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (getting uncomfortable) Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh God Phoebe!! (Phoebe covers one ear.) Oh that's it! That's it! Right there! Oh! (Tries to cover the other ear.) Ooooh-oooh-ooooh... (Covers both ears and continues the massage with her elbows.)
Monica and Chandler's, Chandler has a box of keys and is trying them on the secret closet when someone enters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I wasn't trying to open your closet! I wasn't trying to open your closet! I swear! (Running into the kitchen and sees its Joey.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa, Monica runs a pretty tight ship over here. What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Monica has a secret closet and she won't let me see what's in it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't know! What could she possibly be hiding in here that I can't see?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know. Ooh, I bet it's Richard.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why would Monica be keeping Richard in here?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well off the top of my head uhh, maybe she's having her cake and eating it too. You being the cake and Richard being the too. Or!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And here we go...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I saw this movie once where there was a door and no one knew what was behind it, and when they finally got it open millions and millions and millions of bugs came pouring out and they feasted on human flesh. Y'know it wouldn't kill ya to respect your wife's privacy! (He walks away and into his apartment and looks the door.) Stupid closet full of bugs!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey. Joey, something feels weird and not good weird. I don't-Whoa!! (Winces in pain.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh hey whoa-whoa, don't worry. Okay. When my sisters were pregnant they got every weird feeling in the book, it was always nothing.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Absolutely. But, we're gonna stop by the hospital just to be sure, okay? I'll page Ross on the way. Come on.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Oh God-Ow!-Oo!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay Rach-Rach-Rach look at me, look at me, everything's gonna be fine, trust me. Okay. Take my hand. Here we go. (Rachel grabs his hand.) Oww crushing bones!
The Hospital, Joey is in the waiting room as Rachel comes back out with the doctor.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! So?

lines.speaker: Dr. Long
lines.text: She's fine. She's experiencing Braxton-Hicks contractions, mild discomfort caused by contractions in the uterine wall.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hmm, mild discomfort. So I take it you've had one of these Braxton thingies?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So but everything is normal.

lines.speaker: Dr. Long
lines.text: Absolutely.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And-and there's no danger to her and the baby?

lines.speaker: Dr. Long
lines.text: No-no. Contractions can be unnerving if you don't know what they are, but she's fine.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you doctor. (Dr. Long exits.) (To Joey) Oh thank you for being so nice and calm.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Calm?! I wasn't calm! I've never been more scared in all my life!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But wait you said everything was gonna be okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well what do I know?! I'm not a doctor!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But I-But everything is okay. I'm fine!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You sure?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes! Yes! I got half a mind to contract that doctor's uterus though. Mild discomfort, what's he talking about?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (seeing Ross) Oh hey-hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (rushes in) Is everything okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, everything's fine!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Your page said come to the hospital, what? What was it? What happened?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Something called Braxton-Hicks contractions.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh. Oh. Thank God, most women don't even feel them.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, no uterus, no opinion.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey uh, what's with the candy?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh when you beeped me I was on line at the concession stand at the movie theater.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh you went to the movies by yourself?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No I-Mona!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I gotta go back in there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? Why? What's wrong?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, everything's fine. I just gotta go back...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No really, you tell me what's up.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I-I forgot my underwear. (Ross lets her go.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (To Joey) So, thanks so much for bringing her to the hospital.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh hey, don't worry about it man. Don't worry about it, no big deal.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah but still, I mean it should've been me. I'm the dad.

lines.speaker: Dr. Long
lines.text: (To Joey) Uh, if you have any questions, here's some information on Braxton-Hicks. (Hands Joey a pamphlet.) Oh and by the way, you did the right thing by bringing her in. You're gonna make a wonderful father.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh uh, hey. Not as good as this guy! (Grabs Ross around the shoulders.) He brought Twizlers!
Commercial Break
Monica and Chandler's, Joey and Chandler are still working on the door.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: There has got to be a way!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Easy there Captain Kirk. Oh, do you have a bobby pin?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah. (Checks his hair.) Oh no wait, I'm not an nine-year-old girl.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Then why do you throw like one?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Maybe Monica has a bobby pin.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sure. "Monica."

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, how's the hideously inappropriate crush on Rachel coming?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, really good. Really good. Yeah, I should be ready to kill myself any day now. (Chandler returns with a bobby pin and hands it to Joey.) Wow, you sure found that quick. (He tries the pin in the lock.) I just-I wish I didn't feel this way about Rachel anymore, y'know? I wish things could go back to normal. I mean, I love living with her and God, helping out with the baby is just amazing, but now I think...I think Ross feels left out. Y'know? When I had to take Rachel to the hospital, the doctor thought I was the father. God... You should've seen the look on Ross's face. (Pause) By the way, I have no idea what I'm doing here. For all I know I'm just locking it more. Oh hey, did you try opening it with a credit card?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's a good idea.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. (They switch places and Chandler gets out a credit card.) So uh, Ross is kinda bummed huh?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, I just...I feel terrible.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, it's not your fault. What are you gonna do? Not take her to the hospital? Y'know? You're doing nothing wrong. (Pause) Except for harboring an all consuming love for the woman whose carrying his baby. (He loses his card behind the door.) Richard? If-if you're in there, could you pass me my credit card?
Central Perk, Phoebe is on the couch as Monica enters.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I gotta tell you, yesterday was amazing. That massage felt so good!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No-no, I got that.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So umm, what do you say we make it a weekly appointment?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. Okay but you should know though, I've raised my rates to $200 an hour.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I mean $500.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What's going on?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, you make sex noises when you get massaged!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What? (Laughs.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, and it really freaked me out! And after a while I even tried to hurt you and it just spurred you on.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?! You're crazy! There's nothing sexual about the noises I make!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really? There's nothing sexual about this? (Sexily) Oooh. Oo God! Ohh. Ohhhh. Ohh. (Some cute guy is watching closely.) (To him) What are you looking at?! (Pause) I mean hi.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my-This is so embarrassing. Oh my God, I'm never gonna get massaged again!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, you can't let this stop you from getting massages! No look, I have, I have lots of clients that make the same noises you do!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well not clients, lovers. But let's just y'know, try it again. Come back and-and we'll work through it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I guess.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Great! Okay, if you'll just excuse me. (To the guy) So, did you hear something you liked?
Joey and Rachel's, Rachel is balancing an aluminum can on her stomach as Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey Ross! Check it out! I learned a new trick!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey uh, I brought you some lunch.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh! That's so sweet of you! Oh yum! (Takes a bite out of the sandwich and starts to get sick.) Did you put pickles on this?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well yeah!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh Ross!! (Runs to the bathroom and closes the door.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?! What?! Rach what?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What's going on?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I made her favorite, tuna salad with pickles.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Pickle? Pickles make her sick. Giving her pickles is like giving me salad.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Rachel) I-I'm sorry Rach, I didn't know. Are you gonna be okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I'll be fine. But could someone please make sure that sandwich is gone when I get out there?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm on it! (Takes a bite out of the sandwich.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I can't believe this! I shouldn't be the one making her throw up!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dude relax! It could happen to anyone.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah? Not to you, because you know this stuff. I don't know any of it and I'm the father. I wish I'd be more involved y'know.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) Well, if anyone is keeping score, I no longer eat tuna.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey uh, can I, can I talk to you guys for a second?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What's up?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, sit down. I wanna talk about our situation.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (Gasps) Are you breaking up with us?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: The thing is...'cause I live with Rachel I'm here for a lot of the stuff, okay? (To Rachel) And Ross...Ross is missing everything. So...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Maybe you two should live together.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Are you asking me to move out? Do you not want me here?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh no-no, no-no I love living with you. It just seems that...if you're gonna have a roommate, y'know it might as well be the father.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But Joey, I don't think Ross wants me to move into his apartment and disrupt his life like that. I mean-(Ross turns to her with wishful eyes.)-Or he does.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No I-I-I would love to be around for you and the baby. And we-we can just try it like on a temporary basis.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But Ross, its you and me!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So? Sure! But it-it wouldn't be anything romantic. And I'm-I'm dating Mona-Damnit Mona! I was supposed to meet her like an hour ago! What is wrong with me?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right now, so? What do you think?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know. Is it crazy?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! No it's not. Joey, this is a smart idea.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I was due.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, let's do it. I'll move in.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh Rach that's great. That's great. (They hug and Joey breathes a sigh of relief.)
Monica and Chandler's, Chandler is pounding out the hinge pins on the closet door to get it open.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Not to shabby, I got this all off myself...using my wife's tools. (He takes the door off the frame and we finally get to see what's behind the green door! It is stacked, floor to ceiling, with junk.) Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering) (Gasps) How did you get in there?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (laughs) You're messy.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh no! You weren't supposed to see this!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I married Fred Sanford!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No Chandler, you don't understand! (Chandler starts singing the theme for Sanford and Son, an old TV show starring Redd Fox.) Okay! Okay! Okay! Fine! Now you know. Okay? I'm y'know...I'm sick.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, honey you're not sick! Look, I don't love you because you're organized, I love you in spite of that.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really? You promise you won't tell anyone?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes! And look, now that I know if I got some extra stuff lying around can we, can we share the closet.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well...it's just umm...I'm afraid you might mess it up.
Joey and Rachel's, Joey is drinking a gallon of orange juice as Chandler enters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So Rachel's all moved out huh? How are you taking it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well uh, I wanted to have a few beers, but uh, I got rid of those because Rachel couldn't stand the smell of them. But I have thrown back a lot of orange juice with calcium though. And uh, it's a couple weeks past it's expiration date, so it's got a bit of a kick.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Are you kidding me? I'm great! Yeah, I'm uh; I'm better than great. I am good. And now that she's gone, I can uh, I can do all this stuff around here that I couldn't do before. Y'know? Like umm, I can walk around naked again. Y'know? I can uh, I can watch porn in the living room. Right? This is uh, this is good for me. Y'know? I like being on my own, I'm uh, better off this way. I'm uh, a lone wolf. Y'know? A loner. Alone. All alone. Forever. What's a wolf got to do to get a huh around here?! (Chandler rushes over and hugs him.)
Closing Credits
Phoebe's Apartment, Phoebe is giving Monica another massage.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ohhh.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's it. Just relax.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ohh. Oh! Ohh!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Come on more!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh God!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, you like that don't you?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh yeah right there!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You want it there? You take it there baby!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh Phoebe?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, say my name. Say it! (She stops when she realizes what she's doing.) And now I'm going to cover you back up, and umm we're never doing this again.
End
